rationale:
Regression lens at 4e8a1f45: `go build ./...` is clean (no output, exit 0). `go test ./...` across the full repo shows 42 packages ok and exactly 2 failing tests, both matching the BRIEF's known pre-existing set: internal/harness (`TestLLM_PrecommitReexportComplete`, in-suite stack trace shows the panic surfacing inside `TestLLM_ApprovalGateDiscovery`'s live-agent RunSession/ClaudeCodeAgent.Run call) with `panic: test timed out after 10m0s` — an inherent live-agent-CLI wall-clock limit, not an assertion failure; and internal/instruct `TestRun_IdleNoBeads` failing because the repo's actual current lifecycle state is 111's own in-flight Review Mode ('# MindSpec — Review Mode', Active Spec 111-workflow-panel-runner) rather than idle — exactly the documented z4ps test-isolation/environment-dependence bug, unrelated to this diff's content. No other failures anywhere in the suite.

Pre-existing determination, via source-diff rather than a slow live rerun (the harness suite alone costs ~10min at HEAD): `git diff --name-only a9dab23f 4e8a1f45 -- internal/harness` and `-- internal/instruct` are both EMPTY — neither package's source changed one byte between the merge-base and this commit, so any failure in either is by construction not introduced by 111's diff; it can only be pre-existing/environmental. I also checked the one indirect-dependency risk: internal/harness/sandbox.go imports internal/setup, and internal/setup/claude.go DID change (69 lines, spec 111's own installWorkflows() addition to RunClaude) — but that change is a fast local file-write step (MkdirAll + WriteFile of a JS template), not on any path that touches ClaudeCodeAgent.Run's subprocess I/O; internal/setup's own test package passes (`ok`), and the harness panic's goroutine dump is squarely inside the live-agent subprocess pipe read, unrelated to setup's install logic. internal/validate also changed, but only by adding a new _test.go (ownership_wave2_test.go) — no production-code change, and internal/validate's own tests pass in 194s. Confirmed both flagged failures are pre-existing/environmental, not a regression.
findings: go build ./... clean at 4e8a1f45; no compile errors anywhere in the module.
go test ./... : 42 packages ok, 2 failing tests, matching the BRIEF's documented known-failure set exactly — no new failures.

internal/harness failure is `panic: test timed out after 10m0s` inside ClaudeCodeAgent.Run's live subprocess I/O (goroutine dump shows os/exec Cmd.Start/watchCtx/writerDescriptor stuck on pipe read) — a live-agent wall-clock limit inherent to the scenario harness, not an assertion regression.

internal/instruct TestRun_IdleNoBeads failure is the documented z4ft/z4ps test-isolation issue: the test asserts an idle-template heading but the ambient repo state is 111's own live Review Mode spec, so it renders the review template instead — environment-dependent, not code-dependent.

git diff --name-only a9dab23f 4e8a1f45 -- internal/harness and -- internal/instruct are both empty: zero source changes in either package between merge-base and HEAD, which by itself rules out these two failures being caused by 111's diff.

Only indirect-dependency risk checked: internal/setup/claude.go changed (new installWorkflows() step in RunClaude) and is imported by internal/harness/sandbox.go; internal/validate changed (test-only addition) and is imported by internal/instruct/instruct.go. Neither change is on the failing code path (setup's own tests pass; validate's own tests pass in 194s; the harness panic is in agent-subprocess I/O, unrelated to the fast local file-write installWorkflows adds).
